hooks.command: CMD=$(python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('tool_input',d).get('command',''))" 2>/dev/null || true); case "$CMD" in *grep*|*rg\ *|*ripgrep*|*find\ *|*fd\ *|*ack\ *|*ag\ *)   [ -f graphify-out/graph.json ] &&   echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"graphify: knowledge graph at graphify-out/. For focused questions, run `graphify query \"<question>\"` (scoped subgraph, usually much smaller than GRAPH_REPORT.md) instead of grepping raw files. Read GRAPH_REPORT.md only for broad architecture context."}}'   || true ;; esac

ST=$(python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('tool_input',d).get('subagent_type',''))" 2>/dev/null || true); case "$ST" in Explore|general-purpose)   [ -f graphify-out/graph.json ] &&   echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"graphify: knowledge graph at graphify-out/. Before spawning this exploration agent, try graphify yourself first: run graphify query <seeded with an actual class/function/file name, not a vague phrase> (or graphify explain / graphify path). One good query often returns the whole relevant slice in seconds - cheaper than a subagent trace. Only fall back to this agent if graphify does not surface enough."}}'   || true ;; esac
